mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals stronger manufacturing activity and higher near-term corporate earnings, causing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months with rising INDPRO, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding thirty basis points over three months indicates weakening labor demand that precedes slower consumption and lower corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal Rank IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent negative growth in retail sales and an elevated federal funds rate jointly tighten liquidity conditions and constrain corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days that are larger than either factor alone.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of joint-signal episodes, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an acceleration of consumer price inflation forces the market to revise terminal rate expectations upward, increasing discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered tightening-cycle episodes, or Rank IC < 0.03 during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and forward-looking consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days as cyclical earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of months with rising HOUST, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of months with rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP captures aggregate demand expansion that translates into upward earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting labor income and weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of months with declining PAYEMS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
